description: Machine-readable mapping of coordination surfaces across Ethereum based on interpretive domain grouping, including protocol-aligned primitives, external surfaces, and deprecated abstractions.

surfaces.name: Order Flow Access & Routing
surfaces.description: Access to transaction flow, routing mechanisms, auction surfaces, and user intent entry points.

surfaces.name: Solver Coordination
surfaces.description: Off-protocol coordination of execution strategies, intent resolution, routing, and settlement pathways across competing solvers.

surfaces.name: Sequencing Coordination
surfaces.description: External transaction ordering and sequencing coordination across rollups, shared sequencing systems, ordering rights, and cross-domain execution.

surfaces.name: Builder Coordination
surfaces.description: Coordination of payload construction and proposer-builder interaction, transitioning from market-based framing to protocol-defined roles under ePBS.

surfaces.name: Inclusion
surfaces.description: Constraint mechanisms ensuring transaction inclusion, censorship resistance, and fork-choice enforced inclusion guarantees.

surfaces.name: Commitments & Preconfirmations
surfaces.description: Coordination of early execution guarantees, commitment signaling, pre-reveal semantics, and preconfirmation-adjacent mechanisms prior to final confirmation.

surfaces.name: Proof Generation & Proving Coordination
surfaces.description: External proof generation and proving coordination across zk systems, validity proofs, rollups, proof aggregation, and distributed proving infrastructure.

surfaces.name: Finality
surfaces.description: Consensus-level confirmation and irreversibility of state transitions, including the SSF research direction.

ambiguous_surfaces.name: Execution Coordination (Ambiguous)
ambiguous_surfaces.description: Non-canonical coordination surface spanning routing, solver activity, execution strategy, sequencing, and payload construction.

ambiguous_surfaces.name: Blockspace Allocation (Legacy / Ambiguous)
ambiguous_surfaces.description: Legacy blockspace-market framing increasingly displaced by protocol-native roles, commitments, payloads, inclusion constraints, and narrower execution-resource terminology.

notes: Surfaces are interpretive groupings, not protocol-defined layers.
Surfaces may overlap and interact dynamically.
Some surfaces represent transitional, external, or deprecated abstractions rather than stable protocol primitives.
Execution coordination and blockspace allocation are explicitly tracked as ambiguous and non-canonical surfaces.
Sequencing and proving are represented as external coordination surfaces because they are important adjacent infrastructure categories.
All 12 registry anchors in registry v0.6.4 are represented in this surface map.
